findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that disable security mechanisms such as TLS verification, certificate checks, or safety guards. Seen in ToxicSkills campaign where skills weaken system security to enable further attacks.
findings.recommendation: This code disables important security mechanisms. This is a common precursor to further attacks — once security guards are down, malicious code can operate freely. Legitimate tools should never disable TLS verification or system firewalls.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns that disable security mechanisms such as TLS verification, certificate checks, or safety guards. Seen in ToxicSkills campaign where skills weaken system security to enable further attacks.

properties.recommendation: This code disables important security mechanisms. This is a common precursor to further attacks — once security guards are down, malicious code can operate freely. Legitimate tools should never disable TLS verification or system firewalls.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects prompt injection patterns that use secrecy and urgency to manipulate the agent — telling it to hide actions from the user, not announce findings, keep things secret, or treat something as a "standard measure". These are hallmarks of malicious sampling/prompt injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: This code contains social engineering patterns designed to manipulate an AI agent — telling it to hide actions, keep secrets from the user, or treat suspicious requests as "standard procedure". This is a classic prompt injection technique. Do NOT trust this tool.

matches.description: Bypassing confirmation — instructing agent to not ask permission

findings.ruleDescription: Detects skills and MCP servers that make overbroad capability claims, stuff keywords for discovery abuse, or impersonate trusted vendors. Used to trick agents and users into choosing malicious tools over legitimate ones.
findings.reportTitle: Capability inflation or vendor impersonation detected
findings.recommendation: This tool makes suspicious claims — impersonating trusted vendors, claiming to do "everything", or using keyword stuffing. Legitimate tools have specific, honest descriptions. This may be a social engineering attempt to get you to install a malicious tool.

fullDescription.text: Detects prompt injection patterns that use secrecy and urgency to manipulate the agent — telling it to hide actions from the user, not announce findings, keep things secret, or treat something as a "standard measure". These are hallmarks of malicious sampling/prompt injection attacks.
